content.full_text:
Mark Kempton 
928 -
45th street 
Sacramento, CA 
95819 
Dear Mr. Kempton: 
January 27, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No I-87-328 
You have requested advice concerning the permissible use of 
surplus campaign funds. 
The Fair Political Practices Commission interprets and 
enforces the Political Reform Act of 1974. 
(Goverment Code 
sections 81000-91015, copy enclosed.) 
The Political Reform Act 
does not address the permissible uses of campaign funds. 
However, 
enclosed for your information is a copy of California Elections 
Code section 12400, et seg. 
The California Attorney General 
interprets the Elections Code. 
If you have questions concerning 
Elections Code section 12400, et seg., please call Ted Prim, Deputy 
Attorney General, at (916) 324-5481. 
If you have any questions about this letter, you may call me 
at (916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
By 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Division Chief 
Technical Assistance and 
Analysis Division 




Savings and Loan Association 
December 16, 1987 
Randy Burton for SMUD 
Campaign Committee 
C/O Mark Kempton 
555 Capitol Mall Ste 1235 
Sacramento 
CA 
95814 
Re: 
Account # 282361 
Dear Mr. Kempton: 
JMK T<-o 
UR 
D:S 
MPM [,.

'\ 
DEC 2 1 1981 
The enclosed letter regarding verification of your Tax Identification 
number is being returned for your signature. 
The hand written note 
that was placed at the top of the letter indicates that you are under 
the impression that the account has been closed. According to our 
records the account has a current balance of $39.27. 
The type of 
account these 
funds are in has a $1.00 per month service charge if 
the balance is under $200.00. 
Please come into our office to bring the balance up to $200.00 or to 
close the account since it has been inactive for some time. 
If you 
have any questions, please contact us at 923-4501. 
s~r;ferelY yours, /')

Sales Representitive 
P,O, Box 13326 • Sacramento, CA 95813 • (916) 920-3333 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Mark Kempton 
928 -
45th street 
Sacramento, CA 
95819 
Dear Mr. Kempton: 
December 31, 1987 
Re: 
87-328 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on December 29, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 
18329) .) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
//"\ 
vJeanne Pritchard 
Chief 
Technical Assistance 
Division 
and Analysis 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramenro CA 95H04~0807 • (916) 322~5660

sections.question_synthetic: What are the permissible uses of surplus campaign funds under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that the Political Reform Act does not govern surplus campaign fund usage, and referred the requester to the California Elections Code sections 12400 et seq., which are interpreted by the California Attorney General rather than the FPPC.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document is multi-part containing an initial acknowledgment letter, an unrelated savings and loan bank letter, and the substantive FPPC response. The advisory portion is clear and well-structured. Minor OCR quality issues noted in the S&L letter which is not part of the FPPC response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have requested advice concerning the permissible use of surplus campaign funds.

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act does not address the permissible uses of campaign funds. However, enclosed for your information is a copy of California Elections Code section 12400, et seg. The California Attorney General interprets the Elections Code.

embedding.summary: This file contains FPPC correspondence regarding surplus campaign fund usage, including an acknowledgment letter dated December 31, 1987, and a substantive response dated January 27, 1988, clarifying that the Political Reform Act does not address this matter and referencing Elections Code provisions instead.
